# Peak Fettle — Product Roadmap

*Last updated: May 16, 2026 (v2 — reconciliation pass)*
*Generated by exec-product-manager. Reflects actual shipped state through Phase D + Phase E kickoff.*

---

## Overview

This roadmap is organized into four phases. Phase 1 covers the MVP feature set required for launch. Phase 2 addresses the issues raised during beta testing (synthesized from the four tester personas: Jamie, Marcus, Priya, and Derek). Phase 3 adds growth and differentiation features. Phase 4 captures future-state items that are explicitly deferred post-launch per the product spec.

Beta feedback is tagged inline with the tester persona that surfaced it: **[Jamie]** = casual gym-goer, **[Marcus]** = competitive powerlifter, **[Priya]** = dedicated runner, **[Derek]** = fitness beginner.

**Reconciliation note (2026-05-16):** Phases A–D shipped the majority of Phase 1. This v2 pass updates item status to reflect actual on-disk state, adds three new pre-launch requirements surfaced by exec decisions, and corrects stale product assumptions.

---

## Phase 1 — Core MVP (Launch-Ready Foundation)

These are the features required for a functional v1.0. Nothing ships until all items here are complete.

### 1.1 — Session Logging ✅ COMPLETE

- [x] Workout session logger: sets, reps, weight for weightlifting sessions — **TICKET-017**
- [x] Cardio session logger: split times and total duration for runs, rides, swims — **TICKET-010**
- [x] 1RM estimator (Epley formula) inline-computed from logged sets — **inline Epley, confirmed TYPE-001**
- [x] All session data persisted to database with timestamps — **Phase B migrations**

### 1.2 — Strength Scoring ⚠️ PARTIAL

- [x] DOTS coefficient scoring implemented and transparent — **TICKET-035 (compute_dots_score), TICKET-036 (DOTS_NOTE attribution in API)**
- [ ] **Wilks coefficient scoring** — specified but not confirmed shipped. Verify implementation against published tables before launch.
- [x] Peak Fettle proprietary score — **confirmed equivalent to `percentile_simple` (experience-adjusted percentile), TICKET-031–033**. This is the "where do you rank among people at your level" score. Close original spec item.
- [ ] **User-facing toggle: Wilks / DOTS / PF Score in settings** — not confirmed built. Requires frontend work.
- [ ] **Document PF score formula in user-facing help text** — spec §2.3 transparency requirement. Assign alongside 2.1 glossary work.

**Exec decision (2026-05-16):** `percentile_simple` (experience-adjusted ranking via log-normal model, TICKET-031) satisfies the "Peak Fettle proprietary score" requirement. The DOTS number is exposed via `dots_note` in the API (TICKET-036). Wilks implementation status requires verification.

### 1.3 — Cardio Stats ⚠️ PARTIAL

- [x] Cardio session logging (pace, duration, distance) — **TICKET-010, TICKET-017**
- [ ] **Split times per run segment** — not confirmed. Cardio sets store duration + distance; per-segment splits require schema verification.
- [ ] **Pace trend: rolling 4-week and 12-week windows** — not confirmed built as a UI feature.
- [ ] **Weekly mileage summary for runners and cyclists** — not confirmed. Surface in the analytics/history screen.

**Note:** These items are unblocked for dev but have not been explicitly ticketed. Add to Phase 1 backlog.

### 1.4 — Competitive Percentiles ✅ COMPLETE

- [x] Cohort matching: sport/discipline, age band, sex, experience level — **TICKET-035/038**
- [x] Percentile rank computed and displayed — **TICKET-019/033 (dual-score: experience-adjusted + population)**
- [x] Free-tier — no paywall gate on `/percentile` routes — **TICKET-015 confirmed**
- [x] Cohort transparency: ConfidenceRing shows cohort size + tooltip — **TICKET-039**
- [x] DOTS score exposed alongside percentile — **TICKET-036**
- [ ] **"Your cohort" details screen** — ConfidenceRing tooltip covers the micro-copy, but a dedicated cohort breakdown view ("You're ranked vs. 247 male powerlifters aged 25–34, Intermediate level") is not confirmed built.

### 1.5 — Onboarding & Opening Survey ⚠️ PARTIAL

- [x] Post-registration onboarding: sex + primary discipline — **TICKET-038**
- [x] User constraints (injury/equipment restrictions) captured — **TICKET-012/021**
- [x] Guided first-session flow — **TICKET-005**
- [ ] **Full AI-plan opening survey**: fitness goals, session length preference, weekly availability, preferred exercise types — beyond constraints. The `/plans/generate` endpoint reads constraints + health metrics, but a structured goal/preference survey upstream of plan generation is not confirmed.
- [ ] **Survey editable from settings at any time** — constraints are editable (TICKET-021 profile screen); full survey editability unclear.

### 1.6 — AI-Personalized Plans ✅ COMPLETE

- [x] Claude Haiku 4.5 integration — **Phase C, `routes/plans.js`**
- [x] Structured session + reasoning string returned — **TICKET-011**
- [x] AI plan screen: generate, view, modify — **TICKET-020**
- [x] Plan adaptation: reads user history + constraints + health metrics — **`routes/plans.js`**
- [x] Transparent reasoning: "Why this workout?" expandable per session — **TICKET-011**
- [ ] **Weekly/daily calendar view for the plan** — TICKET-020 has the plan screen, but a calendar-layout view (week grid) is not confirmed as distinct from a list view.
- [ ] **Body composition goal flow**: cut/bulk/recomp target with AI feasibility assessment — not confirmed built.

### 1.7 — Free Tier Static Templates 🔴 NOT BUILT (Pre-Launch Required)

- [x] My Routines strip on home (user-created routines) — **TICKET-003**
- [ ] **🔴 PRE-LAUNCH BLOCKER: Seeded curated template library** (5–6 templates minimum):
  - Push/Pull/Legs 3-day
  - Push/Pull/Legs 6-day
  - Upper/Lower 4-day
  - Beginner Full Body 3-day
  - Intermediate Full Body 3-day (or Bro Split)
  - One cardio-focused template (running beginner plan)
- [ ] **Each template includes**: exercises, sets, reps, rest periods, one-line exercise descriptions (form cues — no video required at launch)
- [ ] **"Recommended for you" filter** on the template library screen based on discipline + experience level from onboarding

**Exec decision (2026-05-16):** Seeded template library is confirmed PRE-LAUNCH REQUIRED. 5–6 templates minimum. Derek persona (beginner) has no starting point without this. Assign as a new ticket — dev estimate: 1–2 days (DB seeding + UI list view). Template screen must exist in Phase E screen layout work (E-005).

### 1.8 — Habit & Streak Tracking ✅ COMPLETE (with one pre-launch addition)

- [x] Streak logic: any logged session counts — **Phase B + TICKET-025**
- [x] Make-up window within same week — **implemented**
- [x] Emergency override (illness/travel/exams) — **manual override flag in DB**
- [x] Streak breaks only on 2 missed sessions + no make-up + no override — **confirmed**
- [x] Current streak, longest streak, weekly completion on home dashboard — **TICKET-016/017**
- [x] Group streak credits — **TICKET-025**
- [ ] **🔴 PRE-LAUNCH REQUIRED: Rest Day Designation** — 3-state streak: logged / intentional rest / missed. See §2.4. Backend ticket queued as dev roadmap item 1.3. **Priya persona is blocked without this** — periodized training plans include scheduled rest days; current system penalizes athletes for following their program.

### 1.9 — User Accounts & Free/Paid Tiers ✅ COMPLETE

- [x] Authentication: sign-up, login, JWT session tokens — **Phase B**
- [x] Password reset — **Phase B `auth.js`**
- [x] AI plans gated behind paid tier — **`routes/plans.js` tier check**
- [x] Free tier: full session logging, percentiles, streak tracking — **confirmed**
- [x] GDPR: data export + account deletion — **TICKET-014, TICKET-030**
- [x] "Your data" screen (export + delete account flow) — **TICKET-021/023**

---

## NEW: Pre-Launch Additions (Exec Decisions 2026-05-16)

These items were not in the original ROADMAP but are confirmed as pre-launch requirements by exec decisions made today.

### PL-1 — Seeded Template Library 🔴 NEW TICKET REQUIRED

*Defined above in §1.7. Restated here for visibility.*

**Owner:** dev-backend (DB seed) + dev-frontend (template browse screen)
**Effort:** S–M (1–2 days)
**Dependencies:** Phase E E-005 (screen layouts) — template browse is one of the 8 primary screens
**Acceptance criteria:**
- 5–6 curated templates seeded in the `exercises` / routines tables
- Template browse screen shows all templates with discipline filter + "recommended for you" surfacing
- Each template has complete exercise list with sets/reps/rest and one-line form cue
- Free-tier users can start a template workout directly from the browse screen
- Beginner Full Body 3-day is the default recommendation for Derek-persona users (beginner + General Strength discipline)

### PL-2 — CSV Import (Garmin / Strava) 🔴 NEW TICKET REQUIRED

*Priya persona pre-launch critical. Bridges wearable gap until TICKET-029 (Garmin OAuth) ships.*

**Owner:** dev-backend + dev-frontend
**Effort:** M (2–3 days)
**Acceptance criteria:**
- User can upload a CSV file from within the app (standard Garmin Connect or Strava export format)
- Import captures: date, activity type (run/ride/swim), duration, distance, pace or split times
- Imported sessions appear in workout history with source labeled "Imported (Garmin)" or "Imported (Strava)"
- Duplicate detection: sessions with the same date + duration + distance are deduplicated on re-import
- Import errors are surfaced as a human-readable list, not a raw error dump
- Imported cardio data is eligible for pace trend calculations once ≥3 sessions are present
- Health data never transmitted to third parties (TICKET-014 privacy architecture applies)

**Note:** This is a file-upload import flow, not an OAuth integration. Full Garmin OAuth stays in TICKET-029 (blocked on dev account). CSV import is the minimum viable bridge for launch.

### PL-3 — Rest Day Designation 🔴 TICKET IN PROGRESS (dev roadmap item 1.3)

*Backend queued. Frontend work needed.*

**Owner:** dev-backend (DB + endpoint) + dev-frontend (streak UI update)
**Effort:** S (1 day backend, 0.5 day frontend)
**Acceptance criteria:**
- Users can log a day as "Intentional Rest Day" from the home screen or log tab
- A rest day counts as streak-preserved (does not count as a missed session)
- The streak display distinguishes logged / rest / missed in the weekly view (3 distinct states, color-coded)
- Streak break logic unchanged: only 2 unexcused missed sessions in a week (rest days and emergency overrides are excused) breaks the streak
- Onboarding copy updated: "Rest days don't break your streak — only back-to-back missed sessions do"

---

## Phase 2 — Beta Fixes & Polish (Pre-Launch Hardening)

These items were surfaced by beta testers. Status updated to reflect current state.

### 2.1 — Terminology & Jargon [🔴 Critical — NOT YET BUILT]
*Flagged by: **[Derek]**, **[Jamie]***

- [ ] **Audit every screen for fitness jargon** — "1RM", "set", "rep", "PR", "progressive overload", "Wilks", "DOTS", "RPE": each must have an inline tooltip or first-use explainer. **Maps to dev roadmap item 1.1 + Phase E E-004 component library (tooltip component)**
- [ ] **Plain-English strength score screen** — "Your strength score is X — this puts you in the top Y% of people at your level" before the formula toggle
- [ ] **Persistent help icon + glossary** — nav-bar accessible glossary with all jargon terms defined
- [ ] **Encouraging percentile opt-in copy** — "See how you compare to people just like you" (already in onboarding, verify copy in TICKET-038)

**Dev dependency:** E-004 (tooltip component in component library). Cannot ship until Phase E E-004 is complete.

### 2.2 — Beginner Onboarding Flow [🔴 Critical — PARTIAL]
*Flagged by: **[Derek]***

- [x] Guided first-session flow — **TICKET-005 (OnboardingPage.qml Qt prototype)**
- [x] Post-registration 2-step onboarding (sex + discipline) — **TICKET-038**
- [ ] **"Day 1" 3-screen React Native intro** — plain-English explanation of what Peak Fettle does before any dashboard. No jargon. TICKET-038 is 2 steps (sex + discipline); a narrative intro before those steps is not built.
- [ ] **Exercise demonstration support in free templates** — description + form cue per exercise (video deferred to Phase 3). Required for template library (PL-1).
- [ ] **Body composition goal screen tone rewrite** — supportive framing: "Here's a plan that will actually work for you" not "That goal isn't realistic". Assign to the AI plans screen (TICKET-020 followup).
- [ ] **Zero-shame streak messaging** — "No worries — your streak resets. Start a new one today." No red alerts, no loss animations. **Maps to dev roadmap item 1.4 (streak messaging overhaul).**

### 2.3 — Strength Scoring Transparency & Depth [🟠 High — PARTIAL]
*Flagged by: **[Marcus]***

- [x] DOTS value exposed in API with attribution — **TICKET-036 (DOTS_NOTE)**
- [x] 1RM calculation method: Epley formula used — confirmed in TYPE-001 and codebase comments
- [x] RPE adjacent: RIR (Reps In Reserve) logging — **TICKET-002** *(Note: RIR is not RPE. RPE is a 1–10 scale; RIR is "reps remaining". Related but distinct. Verify if Marcus's RPE request is satisfied by RIR or needs separate treatment.)*
- [x] Periodization structure in AI plans — `reasoning` field in TICKET-011 references load intent. Verify if "build week / peak week / deload week" labels are explicitly returned or only described in the reasoning string.
- [ ] **Display raw Wilks/DOTS values alongside normalized score** — Wilks implementation unconfirmed; DOTS present in API but UI display needs verification.
- [ ] **Expose 1RM method in settings** — Epley vs. Brzycki toggle with explanation. Not confirmed in settings screen.
- [ ] **Validate Wilks and DOTS against three published reference calculators** — required before launch for credibility with Marcus segment.
- [ ] **RPE field on every set** — if RIR does not satisfy Marcus's request, an explicit RPE (1–10 scale) field is needed. Make a decision before Phase E E-005 (screen layout) freezes the set-logging form.

**Open decision for exec-PM (flag before E-005 screen layout):** Does RIR satisfy the Marcus RPE request, or does a separate RPE field need to be added to the logging form? This affects the set entry form layout.

### 2.4 — Cardio Data & Runner Experience [🔴 Critical — NOT YET BUILT]
*Flagged by: **[Priya]***

- [x] CSV import (Garmin/Strava) — **confirmed pre-launch required (PL-2 above)**
- [x] Rest day designation — **confirmed pre-launch required (PL-3 above)**
- [ ] **Weekly mileage chart with 10% weekly increase warning** — not built. Soft cap + warning (not a hard block). Part of §1.3 cardio stats.
- [ ] **Pace trend monthly roll-up view** — per-session pace is logged; monthly roll-up view not confirmed. Part of §1.3 cardio stats.
- [ ] **Training load indicator** — weekly accumulated stress score (volume × intensity) with plain-English interpretation. Not built. Assign as a Phase 2 ticket.

### 2.5 — Casual User Friction Reduction [🟡 Medium — NOT YET BUILT]
*Flagged by: **[Jamie]***

- [ ] **Survey cap at 5 minutes with progress indicator + "Quick Start" auto-fill path** — relates to §1.5 opening survey. Assign alongside the survey build.
- [ ] **Home dashboard: surface only 2–3 stats for user's primary goal** — advanced stats expandable on tap. Not confirmed. Impacts Phase E E-005 (home screen layout).
- [ ] **Template library "recommended for you" filter** — included in PL-1 scope above.

## Phase 3 — Growth & Differentiation (Post-Launch)

These features make Peak Fettle meaningfully better than alternatives and drive paid conversion. Tackle after the app is stable in production.

### 3.1 — Exercise Demonstration Library
*Currently: form cues (text) are in PL-1 scope. Phase 3 adds animated assets.*
- [ ] Build or license animated GIF / short video clip demonstration library
- [ ] Link every exercise in every template and AI plan to its demonstration
- [ ] Add 3–5 bullet form cue cards per exercise (common mistakes + corrections)

### 3.2 — Social & Community Features
*Currently: group streak credits (TICKET-025) cover friend groups for streaks.*
- [x] Friend group streaks — **TICKET-025 group streak credits**
- [ ] Shareable milestone achievement cards (PR, streak milestone, percentile jump)
- [ ] Friend group leaderboard view (opt-in)

### 3.3 — Advanced Analytics
- [ ] Volume-load tracking over time: total tonnage per muscle group per week
- [ ] Strength balance assessment: push/pull or left/right imbalances
- [ ] Personal record history timeline: visual all-time PR graph per movement

### 3.4 — Coach & Trainer Mode
- [ ] Coach account linked to one or more client accounts
- [ ] Coach can view client progress, annotate sessions, modify AI plans
- [ ] Client receives in-app notification when coach leaves a note

### 3.5 — Data Export
*Currently: GDPR export (`GET /user/data-export`) exists for compliance. Phase 3 adds user-facing export.*
- [x] GDPR data export (compliance baseline) — **TICKET-014**
- [ ] Export session history as formatted CSV
- [ ] Export strength progress chart as shareable PNG
- [ ] Export current AI plan as printable PDF

### 3.6 — Full Garmin / Strava OAuth Integration
*CSV import (PL-2) is the pre-launch bridge. Phase 3 adds native OAuth sync.*
- [ ] Garmin Connect OAuth — **TICKET-029** (blocked on dev account; upgrade from CSV import)
- [ ] Strava OAuth — read runs/rides, two-way sync with Peak Fettle sessions

---

## Phase 4 — Future State (Explicitly Deferred Post-Launch)

Per the product spec, these are acknowledged as future features and are not part of any near-term sprint.

### 4.1 — Full Wearable Integration
*Phase D covers Apple Watch companion (TICKET-028, blocked on dev account) and HealthKit sync (TICKET-022, done). Phase 4 is the beyond-HealthKit items.*
- [x] Apple HealthKit read + write — **TICKET-022 (done)**
- [x] Apple Watch companion (display workout + rest timer) — **TICKET-028 (blocked on dev account)**
- [ ] WHOOP integration — recovery score → AI plan intensity
- [ ] Oura Ring integration — sleep data → training load adjustment
- [ ] Galaxy Watch / Wear OS support

### 4.2 — Nutrition Tracking
- [ ] Basic calorie and macro logging
- [ ] AI-assisted meal suggestions aligned with body composition goal
- [ ] Barcode scanner for food logging

### 4.3 — Video Form Analysis
- [ ] User uploads a short lift video; AI provides automated form feedback
- [ ] Flag common errors with timestamped notes (knee cave, bar path, depth)

| 🔴 Critical | Fitness jargon unexplained throughout app | Derek, Jamie | 🔲 NOT BUILT | 2.1 |
| 🔴 Critical | No "Day 1" beginner onboarding flow | Derek | 🔲 NOT BUILT | 2.2 |
| 🔴 Critical | No wearable/Garmin data import workaround at launch | Priya | 🔴 NEW TICKET PL-2 | 2.4 |
| 🔴 Critical | Rest days penalize periodized athletes | Priya | 🔴 NEW TICKET PL-3 | 2.4 |
| 🔴 Critical | No seeded template library — beginners have no starting point | Derek | 🔴 NEW TICKET PL-1 | 1.7 |
| 🟠 High | Wilks/DOTS not user-verifiable; RPE logging missing | Marcus | ⚠️ PARTIAL | 2.3 |
| 🟠 High | Body composition goal screen tone is clinical, not supportive | Derek | 🔲 NOT BUILT | 2.2 |
| 🟠 High | Streak-break messaging uses shame framing | Derek | 🔲 NOT BUILT | 2.2 |
| 🟡 Medium | AI plan lacks explicit periodization labeling (build/peak/deload) | Marcus | ⚠️ VERIFY | 2.3 |
| 🟡 Medium | Opening survey too long — no quick-start path for casual users | Jamie | 🔲 NOT BUILT | 2.5 |
| 🟡 Medium | Pace trend only shown per-session, not by month | Priya | 🔲 NOT BUILT | 2.4 |
| 🟡 Medium | Home dashboard exposes too much data for casual users | Jamie | 🔲 NOT BUILT | 2.5 |
| 🟡 Medium | No exercise demonstrations in free templates | Derek | Covered in PL-1 (text form cues) | 2.2 |
| 🟢 Low | Free template library needs "recommended for you" filter | Jamie | Covered in PL-1 | 2.5 |
| 🟢 Low | Peak Fettle score formula is opaque | Marcus | ⚠️ Partially covered by DOTS_NOTE; user-facing docs needed | 2.3 |

## Open Product Decisions (Exec Action Required)

These decisions must be made before the indicated dev ticket can be finalized.

| # | Decision | Blocks | Urgency |
|---|---|---|---|
| OD-1 | **RPE vs. RIR**: Does RIR (Reps In Reserve) satisfy Marcus's request for RPE logging, or is a separate RPE (1–10 scale) field required on the set logging form? | Phase E E-005 set-logging screen layout | 🔴 Before E-005 starts |
| OD-2 | **Wilks**: Is Wilks implemented? If not, is it required at launch or deferred to Phase 3? | §1.2 + 2.3 | 🔴 Verify this sprint |
| OD-3 | **AI plan calendar view**: Is a calendar/week-grid layout for AI plans required at launch, or is the list view in TICKET-020 sufficient? | §1.6 | 🟠 Before Phase E E-005 |
| OD-4 | **Body composition goal flow**: Is the cut/bulk/recomp goal-setting flow required at launch, or deferred to Phase 3 alongside the tone rewrite? | §1.6 + 2.2 | 🟠 Decide before launch gate |
